channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's influence spreads through state capitols in both substance and symbolism
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A new year of lawmaking and politicking is underway in U.S. statehouses. Governors are acting quickly to outline their agendas. And in many cases, the influence of President-elect Donald Trump already is rippling through states in both symbolic and substantive ways.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: Some of the same Donald Trump supporters who stormed the U.S. Capitol four years ago are allowed to return to Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about abortion developments in the states and courts as Trump takes office
item.description: Abortion policy could see more changes across the U.S. as President-elect Donald Trump begins his second term and state legislative sessions get rolling.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tens of thousands are expected to converge on Washington for a march days before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight years since its historic first march, the Women's March is returning Saturday to the nation's capital just before President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams, President-elect Trump meet in Florida; Adams says they didn't discuss his legal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Trump in Palm Beach, Florida, on Friday, ahead of Trump's inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Space Force commander nominated to serve as Air Force undersecretary: President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Former fighter pilot and U.S. Space Force commander Matthew Lohmeier will serve as Air Force undersecretary, according to a Truth Social post from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Bakersfield supporters plan to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration
item.description: Also among the attendees will, of course, be Congressman Vince Fong. The 20th District representative is already in the nation's capital. He and those 200 residents are expected to show up on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Justin White, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House reveals Biden's final list of clemency for nearly 2,5000 people, setting new presidential record
item.description: President Biden's final list of commutations was released Friday afternoon, laying out the names and registration numbers of nearly 2,500 inmates whose sentences were reduced by President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court sides with GOP, cancels special election key to House control
item.description: In late December, DFL Gov. Tim Walz set a special election for Jan. 28. But the Supreme Court sided with Republicans who said Walz acted too quickly in setting the date and postponed the election indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Blinken tells AP he's worried Trump administration may abandon key Biden foreign policy initiatives
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken told The Associated Press that he hopes the incoming Trump administration will press forward with key points in President Joe Biden’s foreign policy, including on the Middle East and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yellen says Treasury will use 'extraordinary measures' on Jan. 21 to prevent hitting debt ceiling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In one of her last acts as Treasury Secretary, Janet Yellen said her agency will start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, on January 21 in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday after
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury Department recoups $31 million in improper government payments to dead people
item.description: The Treasury Department announced this week that it had recouped more than $31 million in fraud and improper payments to dead people during just five months of having access to the Social Security Administration's federal death database.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE caucus plans for biggest impact, eyeing key tools to expedite cutting waste
item.description: The DOGE caucus on Thursday discussed strategic ways to address as much government waste as possible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis picks Florida AG Ashley Moody to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Her support for President-elect Donald Trump and legal background is poised to make her a strong ally in the upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court weighs postponing election crucial to Capitol control
item.description: Minnesota Supreme Court justices on Wednesday promised to decide quickly whether a special Roseville-area election critical to deciding power in the House happens as scheduled on Jan. 28 or gets pushed deeper into the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis hopes Trump will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: "There's been no issue that has been higher on the radar," DeSantis added. "We need all hands on deck.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada attorney general moves to protect health care access for DACA recipients
item.description: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is intervening to protect access to health care insurance for Dreamers — the recipients of the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: About 2 in 10 Americans back Hegseth as Trump's Defense secretary: Survey
item.description: In the poll from The Associated Press-NORC Research Center, 19 percent of respondents said that they “strongly approve” or “somewhat approve” the selection of Hegseth by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Thompson, chair of Jan. 6 committee, has talked with White House about pardons
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.), who headed Congress’s special investigation into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, said Tuesday that he has been in talks with the White House about presidential pardons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo raises $3.4 million in 2024 for re-election bid
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is off to a strong start in building campaign funds, raising about $3.4 million over the past year, according to mandatory campaign filing reports.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5
